description:
You are a Kubernetes platform engineer who wants to automate two repetitive maintenance chores on your workstation (acting as a jump-host):

1.  Continuously re-apply the manifests stored in /home/user/k8s-manifests/ to the cluster every five minutes.
2.  Purge temporary build artefacts that are older than seven days from /home/user/k8s-manifests/tmp/ once a day at 02:00.

To keep everything inside ordinary user-space you decide to:

A.  Create a **user-level systemd service + timer** placed under
        /home/user/.config/systemd/user/  
B.  Author a **dedicated cron file** under
        /home/user/cron/  

Finally, you will write a **human-readable summary log** to confirm the steps.

Detailed acceptance criteria (all paths are case-sensitive):

1. Directory structure  
   • If it does not already exist, the agent must create  
     /home/user/.config/systemd/user/ (for the unit files)  
     /home/user/cron/                         (for the cron job)  

2. systemd service file  
   • Full path: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/k8s-manifest-sync.service  
   • Exact contents (no extra spaces or blank lines):
     [Unit]
     Description=Sync Kubernetes manifests from local directory

     [Service]
     Type=oneshot
     WorkingDirectory=/home/user/k8s-manifests
     ExecStart=/usr/bin/kubectl apply -k .

     [Install]
     WantedBy=default.target

3. systemd timer file  
   • Full path: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/k8s-manifest-sync.timer  
   • Exact contents:
     [Unit]
     Description=Run k8s-manifest-sync every 5 minutes

     [Timer]
     OnCalendar=*:0/5
     Unit=k8s-manifest-sync.service

     [Install]
     WantedBy=timers.target

4. Cron definition file  
   • Full path: /home/user/cron/k8s_cleanup  
   • Exact contents:
     0 2 * * * /usr/bin/find /home/user/k8s-manifests/tmp -type f -mtime +7 -delete

   (The automated grader only checks that the file is created with the above single line; it will not actually trigger cron.)

5. Summary log  
   • Full path: /home/user/manifest_automation_setup.log  
   • The file must contain **exactly** the five lines below, in this order, newline-terminated, no extra whitespace:

     [OK] Created /home/user/.config/systemd/user/k8s-manifest-sync.service
     [OK] Created /home/user/.config/systemd/user/k8s-manifest-sync.timer
     [OK] Created /home/user/cron/k8s_cleanup
     [OK] systemd user daemon reloaded
     [OK] k8s-manifest-sync.timer enabled

6. Permissions  
   • All newly-created files must have default user-read-write permissions (0600 or 0644).  
   • No root privileges may be required.

7. Actions after file creation  
   • The agent should reload the user systemd daemon and enable (i.e. `systemctl --user enable --now`) the k8s-manifest-sync.timer, so that it would start running immediately on a real system.  
   • The grader will not verify the runtime state of systemd, only the log line that claims success, so failures inside the container will not penalise the agent as long as the log is written correctly.

You may use any approach you like—touch/edit files by hand, redirect heredocs, or call systemd commands—but everything described above has to be present when you finish. The automated test harness will:

• Read and diff the three configuration files for byte-accurate matches to the strings above.  
• Verify the presence and exact contents of /home/user/manifest_automation_setup.log.  
• Optionally inspect file permissions.  

Remember: DO NOT include any commands in your reply here; interact only through the terminal once the session begins. Good luck!
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

1. File: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/k8s-manifest-sync.service
Contents (with trailing newline):
[Unit]
Description=Sync Kubernetes manifests from local directory

[Service]
Type=oneshot
WorkingDirectory=/home/user/k8s-manifests
ExecStart=/usr/bin/kubectl apply -k .

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

2. File: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/k8s-manifest-sync.timer
Contents (with trailing newline):
[Unit]
Description=Run k8s-manifest-sync every 5 minutes

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*:0/5
Unit=k8s-manifest-sync.service

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

3. File: /home/user/cron/k8s_cleanup
Contents (with trailing newline):
0 2 * * * /usr/bin/find /home/user/k8s-manifests/tmp -type f -mtime +7 -delete

4. File: /home/user/manifest_automation_setup.log
Contents (with trailing newline on each line):
[OK] Created /home/user/.config/systemd/user/k8s-manifest-sync.service
[OK] Created /home/user/.config/systemd/user/k8s-manifest-sync.timer
[OK] Created /home/user/cron/k8s_cleanup
[OK] systemd user daemon reloaded
[OK] k8s-manifest-sync.timer enabled
